summary: R3 architecture review accepts the M2 route transport implementation. The change keeps transport metadata under explicit state.m2.transport schema, exposes route preview through a read-only protocol query, preserves sim-core isolation from UI/host APIs, and uses deterministic integer seasonal route evaluation with stable tie-breakers. The known M2 save/load omission remains a non-blocking follow-up because this task intentionally did not touch packages/save-format.

tests_run.result: Reported expected uncommitted implementation changes in packages/protocol, packages/sim-core, tests, and allowed project orchestration/message paths; no forbidden app/client/desktop paths were listed.

tests_run.command: node -e "const fs=require('node:fs'); const p='project/tasks/active/M2-ROUTE-TRANSPORT-001.json'; const task=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(p,'utf8')); const required=['id','summary','owner_role','reviewer_role','status','scope','acceptance_criteria','required_tests','risk_level','branch_or_worktree']; for (const key of required) { if (!(key in task)) throw new Error('missing '+key); } if (task.id !== 'M2-ROUTE-TRANSPORT-001') throw new Error('bad id'); if (!Array.isArray(task.scope.allowed_paths) || !Array.isArray(task.scope.forbidden_paths)) throw new Error('bad scope'); console.log('M2-ROUTE-TRANSPORT-001 task JSON shape ok');"

tests_run.result: Vitest passed: 9 test files, 45 tests. Includes tests/sim-core-m2-route-transport.test.ts proving seasonal road/river choice changes, capacity-exceeded preview, duplicate route edge invariant, and no state hash mutation from preview queries.
Vitest passed: 1 test file, 3 tests. Includes parseGameQueryV1 acceptance/rejection coverage for sim.preview-m2-transport-route payloads.
Node and worker-compatible hashes matched: hello 61e0ba1c, command/query d275faa4, save/load 28074edd; save/load byte length 10920.
Architecture boundaries passed; determinism passed; root Vitest passed 16 files/79 tests; web Vitest passed 1 file/3 tests.
format:check, lint, recursive typecheck, and architecture:check passed.

decisions: Accepted state.m2.transport shape for this milestone: it is explicit, versioned with schemaVersion 1, canonicalized by stable numeric IDs, and included in canonicalWorldStateV0Text/stateHash.
Accepted the protocol surface as a read-only GameQueryV1 addition. It does not introduce a separate player/AI command path and it returns copied read-model fields rather than mutable WorldState.

Accepted the deterministic route selection model for M2: integer BPS formulas, sorted directed edges, and tie-breakers by total cost, edge count, route ID sequence, direction, then destination district ID.
Accepted the canonical M2 boot hash change to 92903cba because transport routes, district seasonality, and monthly curves are now authoritative M2 state and are covered by boot-boundary tests.

No new ADR is required for this task because the implementation follows the existing approved Worker-authoritative TypeScript sim-core/protocol architecture and does not change frozen product, platform, save-format, IPC, dependency, or host-boundary decisions.
risks: M2 save/load still omits state.m2 through packages/save-format. This remains non-blocking for M2-ROUTE-TRANSPORT-001 because save-format was out of scope, but it must be handled before relying on save/load for M2 transport state.
Transport preview does not commit stock movement and should not be treated as an authoritative transfer command by later UI or AI work.

Seasonal cost/capacity formulas are fixture-scale M2 assumptions. If future content allows very large base costs/capacities or route counts, add bounded numeric schema limits and measured performance evidence before widening usage.
requested_action: Proceed with lead integration gates for M2-ROUTE-TRANSPORT-001; keep the M2 save/load omission as an explicit follow-up risk.
